description: Schema for cloud infrastructure metrics time-series dataset

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp for the observation (hourly granularity)

resource_id.description: Unique resource identifier from CUR data (e.g., i-0000000003e9, arn:aws:rds:...)

resource_type.description: Classified resource type derived from CUR product_code and usage_type

account_name.description: Linked AWS account name from CUR

cpu_percent.description: CPU utilization percentage [0-100]. Generated via Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process with cost-derived baseline.

cpu_utilization_hourly.description: JSON array of 12 five-minute CPU utilization samples within the hour. Provides sub-hourly granularity for detailed analysis.

memory_mib.description: Memory usage in MiB. Generated via Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process.

network_in_bytes.description: Inbound network traffic in bytes per hour. Generated via Geometric Brownian Motion.

network_out_bytes.description: Outbound network traffic in bytes per hour. Generated via Geometric Brownian Motion.

disk_io_ops.description: Disk I/O operations per hour. Generated via Geometric Brownian Motion.

database_connections.description: Active database connections. Generated via Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process.
database_connections.null_condition: null for non-database and non-cache resources

gpu_utilization.description: GPU utilization percentage [0-100]. Generated via Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process.

label.description: Classification label assigned AFTER metric generation. Distribution target: normal ~87%, benign ~7%, anomaly ~6%.

event_type.description: Specific event type when label != 'normal'. Examples: memory_leak, ddos_spike, db_saturation, scheduled_backup, autoscaling, etc.

generation_methodology.baseline_derivation: Metric baselines are probabilistically estimated from CUR cost data, NOT from labels

stochastic_processes.ornstein_uhlenbeck: Used for mean-reverting metrics (CPU, memory, connections)
stochastic_processes.geometric_brownian_motion: Used for multiplicative/growth metrics (network, disk IO)
stochastic_processes.poisson: Used for burst events and spike counts

generation_methodology.anomaly_generation: Anomalies emerge from realistic failure mechanisms applied to the natural time series
generation_methodology.label_assignment: Labels assigned AFTER complete metric generation based on injected events

leakage_prevention.no_label_encoding: Metrics are never generated from labels
leakage_prevention.distribution_overlap: Benign and anomaly distributions intentionally overlap
leakage_prevention.normal_spikes: Normal data contains random spikes (0.5% of hours)
leakage_prevention.extreme_benign: 20% of benign events have amplified effects
